title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: The present ages of P, Q and R are 24 years, 30 years and 36 years respectively. What was the ratio of their ages 6 years ago?
questions.explanation: Given: The present ages of P, Q and R are 24 years, 30 years and 36 years respectively. Solution: Present ages: P = 24, Q = 30, R = 36 Ages 6 years ago P = 24 − 6 = 18 Q = 30 − 6 = 24 R = 36 − 6 = 30 So the ratio becomes: 18 : 24 : 30 Divide by the common factor 6: 18 ÷ 6 : 24 ÷ 6 : 30 ÷ 6 = 3 : 4 : 5 So, 3 : 4 : 5 was the ratio of their ages 6 years ago. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: What will come in the place of ‘?’ in the following equation, if ‘+’ and ‘-‘ are interchanged and ‘x’ and ‘÷’ are interchanged? 15 ÷ 6 + 14 x 2 - 1 = ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Equation: 15 ÷ 6 + 14 × 2 − 1 Interchange: + ↔ − × ↔ ÷ ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[], {}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\\hline \text{Addition} &+ \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​​​ Step-by-step: 15 ÷ 6 + 14 × 2 − 1 → 15 × 6 − 14 ÷ 2 + 1 Now calculate: 15 × 6 = 90 14 ÷ 2 = 7 So, 90 − 7 + 1 = 84 Final Answer: 84 Final Correct Option: (B)

questions.question: Find the ODD one out. Mumbai, Kolkata, Pune, Chennai, Delhi
questions.explanation: Given: Mumbai, Kolkata, Pune, Chennai, Delhi Mumbai – Capital of Maharashtra Kolkata – Capital of West Bengal Chennai – Capital of Tamil Nadu Delhi – National Capital of India Pune – Not a state capital (Mumbai is the capital of Maharashtra) All except Pune are capitals. So, Pune is odd one out. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All horses are elephants. Some horses are giraffes. No elephant is a tiger. Conclusions: (I) Some elephants are giraffes. (II) Some giraffes are tigers.
options: Neither Conclusion (I) nor Conclusion (II) is true
questions.explanation: Given: ​Statements: All horses are elephants. Some horses are giraffes. No elephant is a tiger. From the given statements Venn diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b84f3170-5a06-4590-9cb6-2a57c4456ba5.png) (I) Some elephants are giraffes.​ All horses are elephants → those horses are elephants. =&gt Some elephants are giraffes ✔ (Conclusion I true) (II) Some giraffes are tigers. Giraffes linked with elephants cannot be tigers. =&gt “Some giraffes are tigers” ✘ not possible. Final Answer: Only Conclusion (I) follows. Final Correct Option: (D)

questions.question: Address 1: Mr. Rawesh Kumar, 12/5, Palm Grove, Mumbai - 400017 Address 2: Mr. Raw Kumar, 12/5, Palm Grove Society, Mumbai - 400017 Are these likely to be the same?
options: No, the house numbers do not match
No, the society name is different
questions.explanation: The house number, road name, and postal code match exactly. The only difference is the name and society name is very close to same. So, Correct Answer: C) No, the names are different

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All giraffes are bees. No bee is a road. No bee is a fly. Conclusions: (I) No giraffe is a road. (II) Some flies are roads.
questions.explanation: Given: ​Statements: All giraffes are bees. No bee is a road. No bee is a fly. From the given statements Venn diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d43a308d-d304-4908-af34-4eceaa9f865c.png) Conclusions: (I) No giraffe is a road. From (1): All giraffes are bees. From (2): No bee is a road. =&gt Giraffes (being bees) cannot be roads. So No giraffe is a road ✔ Conclusion II: Some flies are roads. No bee is fly, but relation between flies and roads is not given. =&gt Cannot conclude Some flies are roads ✘ Final Answer: Only conclusion (I) follows. Final Correct Option: (A) ​

questions.question: The present ages of a mother and her daughter are 72 years in total. 8 years ago, the ratio of their ages was 5 : 1. What will be the ratio of their ages after 8 years from now?
questions.explanation: Given: The present ages of a mother and her daughter are 72 years in total. 8 years ago, the ratio of their ages was 5 : 1. Solution: Let present ages of Mother = M and Daughter = D. Total present age: M + D = 72 Ratio 8 years ago 8 years ago: Mother = (M - 8) Daughter = (D - 8) Given ratio (5 : 1) ​M−8D−8\frac{M-8}{D-8} D−8M−8​ = 5 M - 8 = 5(D - 8) M - 8 = 5D - 40 M = 5D - 32 Substitute in total age: (5D - 32) + D = 72 6D - 32 = 72 6D = 104 D = 1046\frac{104}{6}6104​​ = 1713\frac{1}{3}31​​​ Mother: M = 72 - 1713\frac{1}{3}31​​ = 5423\frac{2}{3}32​​​ Ages after 8 years Mother: 5423\frac{2}{3}32​​ + 8 = 6223\frac{2}{3}32​​​ Daughter: 1713\frac{1}{3}31​​ + 8 = 2513\frac{1}{3}31​​​ Ratio: 6223\frac{2}{3}32​​ : 2513\frac{1}{3}31​​​ Multiply by 3: 188 : 76 Divide by 4: 47 : 19 So, 47 : 19 will be the ratio of their ages after 8 years from now. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘finish your work’ is coded as ‘ld fa mo’ and ‘work really hard’ is coded as ‘mr ta ld’. How is ‘work’ coded in that language?
questions.explanation: Information Given: finish your work → ld fa mo work really hard → mr ta ld Formula Used: Common word → common code. Step-by-step: Common word = work Common code in both codes = ld Thus work → ld. Final Answer: ld Final Correct Option: (B)

questions.question: In the word "BIOLOGY", what is the alphabetical index of the 3rd letter from the right?
questions.explanation: Given: BIOLOGY ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/3dd88c92-a4ff-4f84-bbfb-cd3e5546ca54.png) From right side: Y (1st) G (2nd) O (3rd) The 3rd letter from the right is O. (O = 15) So, 15 is the alphabetical index of the 3rd letter from the right. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong to the group based on meaning? PEN, PENCIL, ERASER, BOOK, TIGER
questions.explanation: Given: PEN, PENCIL, ERASER, BOOK, TIGER Let us analyze: Pen → Stationery item Pencil → Stationery item Eraser → Stationery item Book → Used for study (related to stationery) Tiger → Animal The first four are related to study or stationery. So, Tiger is an animal and unrelated to the group. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the thirdnumberin the same way as the secondnumberis related to the firstnumber. 529: 22 :: 100: ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: 529 : 22 :: 100 : ? Logic: Second number = √(First number) − 1 Step-by-step: ​529 : 22 529 → √529 = 23 23 − 1 = 22 Apply 100 : ?​ √100 = 10 10 − 1 = 9 Final Answer: 9 Final Correct Option: (C)

questions.question: If 15 March 2015 was a Sunday, then what day of the week was 15 July 2015?
questions.explanation: Given: If 15 March 2015 was a Sunday. Solution: We count the days from 15 March to 15 July 2015. March → 31 − 15 = 16 days April = 30 days May = 31 days June = 30 days July → up to 15 = 15 days Total days: 16 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 15 = 122 days Now divide by 7: 122 ÷ 7 = 17 weeks + 3 days So the day shifts 3 days forward from Sunday. Sunday → Monday (1) → Tuesday (2) → Wednesday (3) So, Wednesday of the week was 15 July 2015. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: The ratio of the present ages of A and B is 5 : 3. After 8 years from now, A’s age will be 28 years. What will be the ratio of the ages of A and B after 12 years from now?
questions.explanation: Given: The ratio of the present ages of A and B is 5 : 3. After 8 years from now, A’s age will be 28 years. Solution: Let present ages be: A = (5x) B = (3x) Condition after 8 years After 8 years, A’s age = 28 5x + 8 = 28 Solving: (5x = 20) (x = 4) Present ages: A = (5 × 4 = 20) B = (3 × 4 = 12) Ages after 12 years: A = (20 + 12 = 32) B = (12 + 12 = 24) Ratio after 12 years: (32 : 24) Divide by 8: (4 : 3) So, 4 : 3 will be the ratio of the ages of A and B after 12 years from now. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: If ‘+’ means ‘−’, ‘−’ means ‘×’, ‘×’ means ‘÷’ and ‘÷’ means ‘+’, then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 112 × 4 ÷ 8 − 2 + 23 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: 112 × 4 ÷ 8 − 2 + 23 = ? Given Sign + - × ÷ New Sign - × ÷ + Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​​​ New equation: 112 ÷ 4 + 8 × 2 - 23 = ? 28 + 8 × 2 - 23 = ? 28 + 16 - 23 = ? 44 - 23 = ? ? = 21 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which of the following options can replace the? 417, 413, 418, 415, 421, ?
questions.explanation: Given: ​417, 413, 418, 415, 421, ? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/1f87d8e1-8e37-4032-ac34-4e845ababf1b.png) So, the next number is: 419 Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: No bag is a wallet. All wallets are purses. Some purses are organisers. Conclusions: (I) No purse is a bag. (II) Some wallets are organisers.
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: Given: ​Statements: No bag is a wallet. All wallets are purses. Some purses are organisers. From the given statements Venn diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/8cf421d5-a6ba-4fc8-bb6f-9e99cbcae11a.png) I: No purse is a bag. Given: No bag is wallet and wallets ⊆ purses. But purses may exist that are bags (not wallets). =&gt Cannot be Confirm ✘ II: Some wallets are organisers. Given: Some purses are organisers, but wallets are only a part of purses. No guarantee those purses are wallets. =&gt Does not Follow. Final Answer: Neither conclusion follows. Final Correct Option: (A)

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? SWE, ZDL, GKS, NRZ, ?
questions.explanation: Given: SWE, ZDL, GKS, NRZ, ? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/e82e4d29-1d6d-4bc2-ae75-de932f5cee1a.png) Logic: Letters are increasing + 7 place. S + 7 = Z, Z + 7 = G, G + 7 = N, N + 7 = U W + 7 = D, D + 7 = K, K + 7 = R, R + 7 = Y E + 7 = L, L + 7 = S, S + 7 = Z, Z + 7 = G So, the missing term is UYG. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the given sets. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/deleting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (11, 3, 70) (9, 2, 55)
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 12 18 6 24 ? 30
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: 12 18 6 24 ? 30 Logic: Two alternating series. Explanation: Odd positions: 12, 6, ? 12 − 6 = 6 6 − 6 = 0 Even positions: 18, 24, 30 18 + 6 = 24 24 + 6 = 30 Thus missing term = 0 Short Trick: Split into two series (odd & even positions). Final Answer: 0 Final Correct Option: (D)

questions.question: Pointing to a man in a photograph, Anwali said, "His mother is the only daughter of my mother's father." How is the man in the photograph related to Anwali?
questions.explanation: Given: Pointing to a man in a photograph, Anwali said, "His mother is the only daughter of my mother's father." ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0edda6e0-11bd-4b40-b834-551dd3a19334.png) From the given information blood relation diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d6bfce04-5cd9-4a8d-9594-b7ada9fa54e8.png) So, the man in the photograph is the brother of Anwali. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: If in a certain code, 'MANGO' is written as 'LKZYMLFENM', what is the code of 'GUITAR'?
questions.explanation: Given: If in a certain code, 'MANGO' is written as 'LKZYMLFENM'. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0f9f032b-5afd-4f62-8833-de0e4f7748d1.png) Logic: Each letter is replaced by the two previous letters in reverse order. For, MANGO = LKZYMLFENM M → L K A → Z Y N → M L G → F E O → N M Similarly, GUITAR = ? G → F E U → T S I → H G T → S R A → Z Y R → Q P So combine: F E T S H G S R Z Y Q P So, GUITAR is written as FETSHGSRZYQP. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 7214365 is arranged in the ascending order from left to right. The position(s) of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?
questions.explanation: Given: 7214365 Arrange the digits in ascending order (smallest to largest).​ Given Number 7 2 1 4 3 6 5 Ascending Order 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 Unchanged positions: 2, 4, 6 Total = 3 digits So, the position(s) of three digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Based on the alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the letter-cluster that does not belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their positions in the letter- cluster.)
questions.explanation: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/325d75c3-55e2-4ac8-9360-452770a0494b.png) Logic: First → Second (−6), Second → Third (−4). A: QKG Q→K = −6 K→G = −4 B: TNK T→N = −6 N→K = −3 ✘ C: ZTP Z→T = −6 T→P = −4 D: WQM W→Q = −6 Q→M = −4 ✔ Thus only B does not follow the pattern. Final Answer: TNK Final Correct Option: (B)

questions.question: What will come in the place of the question mark (?) in the following equation, if ‘ −’ and ‘+’ are interchanged and ‘÷’ and ‘×’ are interchanged? 200 × 25 + 30 − 5 ÷ 10 = ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Equation: 200 × 25 + 30 − 5 ÷ 10 = ? Interchange: + ↔ − × ↔ ÷ ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[], {}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\\hline \text{Addition} &+ \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​​​ za Now calculate: 200 ÷ 25 = 8 5 × 10 = 50 So, 8 − 30 + 50 = 28 Final Answer: 28 Final Correct Option: (B)

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 45 46.6 48.2 49.8 ? 53
questions.explanation: Information Given: 45, 46.6, 48.2, 49.8, ?, 53 Logic: Constant increase of +1.6 each step. Step-by-step: 46.6 − 45 = 1.6 48.2 − 46.6 = 1.6 49.8 − 48.2 = 1.6 Next term: 49.8 + 1.6 = 51.4 Check: 51.4 + 1.6 = 53 Final Answer: 51.4 Final Correct Option: (C)
